description: X-ray and electron diffraction pattern characteristics of quasicrystals and related structures
key_principle: Quasicrystals show sharp Bragg peaks (like crystals) but with irrational peak spacing ratios (unlike crystals). The existence of sharp peaks despite forbidden symmetry axes was the original experimental signature.

quasicrystal.peak_ratio: irrational (τ = φ ≈ 1.618 for icosahedral/decagonal; 1+√2 for octagonal)

icosahedral_6D.description: 6 integers (n1..n6) needed to label each Bragg peak of an icosahedral QC
icosahedral_6D.basis_vectors: 6 vectors along icosahedral 5-fold axes in 6D embedding space
icosahedral_6D.reduction_to_3D: Project 6D reciprocal lattice onto 3D physical space

decagonal_5D.description: 5 integers needed: 4 for in-plane quasiperiodic dimensions + 1 for periodic stacking axis
decagonal_5D.in_plane_indexing: 4 integers with basis rotated by 36° steps

penrose_4D.description: 4 integers needed to index 2D Penrose diffraction pattern
penrose_4D.basis_vectors: 4 vectors at 72° intervals in 4D space projected to 2D

materials.peak_ratio_example: Adjacent principal peaks along 5-fold axis: ratio ≈ τ = 1.618
materials.bragg_peak_density: Countably infinite, dense on real line
key_diffraction_features: 10 bright spots in 5-fold zone axis pattern (SAED)
Pattern indistinguishable from crystal at first glance but cannot be indexed with 3 integers
Concentric rings of 10 spots along 5-fold axis
6 spots along 3-fold axis, 4 along 2-fold axis (full icosahedral symmetry)
materials.original_experiment: Selected area electron diffraction (SAED) in TEM by Shechtman 1982/1984
materials.notes: Shechtman's original 1984 pattern showed 10 sharp spots with icosahedral arrangement; metastable phase

materials.peak_ratio_example: Peak spacing ratio along 5-fold axis ≈ φ = 1.618
key_diffraction_features: Peaks sharper than Al-Mn due to equilibrium phase
Full icosahedral Ih symmetry in all zone axes
materials.notes: First thermodynamically stable icosahedral QC; X-ray peaks as sharp as good crystals

materials.peak_ratio_example: In-plane peak ratios ≈ φ; out-of-plane (stacking): integer ratios
materials.bragg_peak_density: Dense in quasiperiodic plane, discrete along stacking axis
key_diffraction_features: 10-fold symmetric ring pattern in zone axis along stacking direction
Standard periodic pattern perpendicular to stacking axis
Clear separation of quasiperiodic and periodic dimensions
materials.notes: Model system for decagonal QC; atomic structure studied by X-ray and neutron diffraction

key_diffraction_features: 12 bright spots in 12-fold zone axis pattern
Both triangular and square sub-lattice features
materials.notes: First dodecagonal QC; thin-film specimen; also found in soft matter systems

key_diffraction_features: 8 bright spots in 8-fold zone axis pattern
Ammann bar pattern dual to tiling
materials.notes: Octagonal QC analog to Ammann-Beenker tiling

selected_area_electron_diffraction.advantage: Sub-micron area; can probe single grains; showed original Shechtman result
selected_area_electron_diffraction.limitation: Dynamical diffraction effects in thick samples

x_ray_powder_diffraction.advantage: Bulk average; well-established peak positions and intensities
x_ray_powder_diffraction.limitation: Needs crystallites; poor for metastable phases

high_resolution_tem.advantage: Direct imaging of atomic columns; reveals local structure

mathematical_background.quasiperiodic_fourier: Fourier transform of quasiperiodic function has pure point spectrum (sharp peaks) at positions that are integer linear combinations of incommensurable basis vectors
mathematical_background.number_of_basis_vectors: For D-dimensional quasicrystal: need N > D incommensurate basis vectors
mathematical_background.shechtman_formula_reference: D. Shechtman, I. Blech, D. Gratias, J.W. Cahn, Phys. Rev. Lett. 53, 1951 (1984)
